stage.name: Swapping & Bridging (Elementary)
stage.description: What swapping and bridging are, CEX vs DEX swaps, how bridges work, and the risks beginners must know.

elements.content: Trading one crypto for another — no bank required

Swapping simply means exchanging one cryptocurrency for another. You have Bitcoin and want Ethereum? You swap. You have USDC and want XRP? You swap.


There are two ways to swap: through a centralized exchange (CEX) like Coinbase where a company matches buyers and sellers, or through a decentralized exchange (DEX) where code does it automatically with no middleman.

actions.text: 🔄 What is Swapping?
Trading one crypto for another — no bank required

Swapping simply means exchanging one cryptocurrency for another. You have Bitcoin and want Ethereum? You swap. You have USDC and want XRP? You swap. There are two ways to swap: through a centralized exchange (CEX) like Coinbase where a company matches buyers and sellers, or through a decentralized exchange (DEX) where code does it automatically with no middleman
💱 Swap. Exchange one crypto for another
🏦 CEX Swap. Company matches the trade
🤖 DEX Swap. Code executes automatically
⚡ Speed. Usually seconds to complete

elements.content: Two ways to swap — what is the real difference?

CEX swap — you log into Coinbase, Binance, or Kraken and click swap. Easy. But the exchange holds your funds during the trade. You need an account and ID verification. Fees are usually clear upfront.


DEX swap — you connect your own wallet (like MetaMask) to a protocol like Uniswap. No account needed. You stay in control of your funds the entire time. The trade executes via smart contract. The XRPL has a built-in DEX — one of the oldest in crypto.

actions.text: 🏦 CEX vs DEX Swaps
Two ways to swap — what is the real difference?

CEX swap — you log into Coinbase, Binance, or Kraken and click swap. Easy. But the exchange holds your funds during the trade. You need an account and ID verification. Fees are usually clear upfront. DEX swap — you connect your own wallet (like MetaMask) to a protocol like Uniswap. No account needed. You stay in control of your funds the entire time. The trade executes via smart contract. The XRPL has a built-in DEX — one of the oldest in crypto
🏦 CEX. Exchange holds your funds
🔓 DEX. Your wallet, your control
📋 CEX. ID required, easy UI
🔗 DEX. Wallet only, no account needed

elements.content: Moving crypto from one blockchain to another

Different blockchains are like different countries — they each have their own currency and rules and they do not automatically talk to each other. Bitcoin cannot natively move to Ethereum. ETH cannot natively move to Solana.


A bridge is a protocol that connects two blockchains. You lock your tokens on Chain A, and the bridge mints an equivalent on Chain B. When you are done, you burn the tokens on Chain B and unlock your originals on Chain A.

🪙 Mint
Wrapped token created on Chain B

🔓 Unlock
Original tokens released on Chain A

actions.text: 🌉 What is a Bridge?
Moving crypto from one blockchain to another

Different blockchains are like different countries — they each have their own currency and rules and they do not automatically talk to each other. Bitcoin cannot natively move to Ethereum. ETH cannot natively move to Solana. A bridge is a protocol that connects two blockchains. You lock your tokens on Chain A, and the bridge mints an equivalent on Chain B. When you are done, you burn the tokens on Chain B and unlock your originals on Chain A
🔒 Lock. Tokens locked on Chain A
🪙 Mint. Wrapped token created on Chain B
🔥 Burn. Return wrapped token to bridge
🔓 Unlock. Original tokens released on Chain A

elements.content: The mistakes that cost people money — and how to avoid them

Wrong network — the most common beginner mistake. Sending ETH on the Ethereum network to an address expecting ETH on Arbitrum. The funds appear to vanish. Always confirm the network before sending.


Slippage — your swap executes at a slightly worse price than shown because the market moved. Set slippage tolerance carefully.


Bridge hacks — bridges hold large amounts of locked tokens, making them high-value targets. Over $2B was stolen from bridges in 2022 alone. Use only well-established, audited bridges.

🌐 Wrong Network
Always confirm chain before sending

📉 Slippage
Price moves between click and confirm

actions.text: ⚠️ Swap & Bridge Risks for Beginners
The mistakes that cost people money — and how to avoid them

Wrong network — the most common beginner mistake. Sending ETH on the Ethereum network to an address expecting ETH on Arbitrum. The funds appear to vanish. Always confirm the network before sending. Slippage — your swap executes at a slightly worse price than shown because the market moved. Set slippage tolerance carefully. Bridge hacks — bridges hold large amounts of locked tokens, making them high-value targets. Over $2B was stolen from bridges in 2022 alone. Use only well-established, audited bridges
🌐 Wrong Network. Always confirm chain before sending
📉 Slippage. Price moves between click and confirm
🔓 Bridge Hack. Bridges are high-value targets
✅ Safety. Use only established, audited bridges

questions.question: What is swapping in crypto?

options.label: Moving funds from a hot wallet to a cold wallet

questions.analysis: Swapping means trading one crypto directly for another — for example, exchanging ETH for USDC. It can be done on a CEX (like Coinbase) or a DEX (like Uniswap) where a smart contract executes the trade automatically.

questions.question: What does a bridge do?

options.label: Connects a crypto wallet to a bank account
Speeds up transactions on a single blockchain
Moves tokens between two different blockchains

questions.analysis: Blockchains are separate networks that don't communicate natively. A bridge locks your tokens on Chain A and mints equivalent tokens on Chain B, allowing you to use your assets on a different network.

questions.question: On a DEX swap, who holds your funds during the trade?

options.label: No one — the smart contract executes it and you keep custody

questions.analysis: DEX swaps are trustless. You connect your own wallet, the smart contract executes the trade, and you receive the new token directly. No company ever holds your funds — unlike a CEX where the platform has custody.

questions.question: What is the most common beginner mistake when bridging?

options.label: Choosing a DEX instead of a CEX

questions.analysis: The most common expensive mistake: sending ETH on Ethereum mainnet to an address that expects ETH on Arbitrum. The funds appear lost because the receiving wallet isn't watching the right network. Always verify the network on both the sending and receiving side before confirming.

questions.question: Why are bridges high-value hack targets?

options.label: They charge the highest swap fees
They are built on outdated technology
They lock large amounts of tokens in smart contracts

questions.analysis: Bridges aggregate enormous amounts of locked tokens — making them attractive targets. The Ronin bridge held $625M when hacked. The Wormhole bridge had $320M stolen. Only use bridges that are audited, battle-tested, and widely used by the community.
